heroDescription: Discover everything you need to know about this charming crossbreed between Cocker Spaniels and Poodles. From their loving personality to essential care tips.
aboutDescription: The Cockapoo is a delightful crossbreed that combines the best traits of both Cocker Spaniels and Poodles. Known for their intelligence, affectionate nature, and low-shedding coat, they make excellent family pets.
originText: First bred in the 1960s in the United States
personalityDescription: Cockapoos are known for their wonderful temperament and adaptability. They inherit the best qualities from both parent breeds.
friendlyText: Excellent with children and other pets
intelligentText: Easy to train and eager to please
careDescription: Proper care and grooming are essential for keeping your Cockapoo healthy and happy. Their coat requires regular maintenance.
groomingText: Brush 2-3 times per week to prevent matting
feedingDescription: A balanced diet is crucial for your Cockapoo's health and wellbeing. Follow these guidelines for optimal nutrition.
puppyFeedingText: 3-4 small meals per day with high-quality puppy food
adultFeedingText: 2 meals per day, 1/2 to 1 cup of dry food total
treatsText: Limit treats to 10% of daily caloric intake
trainingDescription: Cockapoos are intelligent and eager to please, making them relatively easy to train with consistent, positive methods.
basicCommandsText: Start with sit, stay, come, and down commands
socializationText: Expose to various people, animals, and environments
breedOverviewDescription: Comprehensive information about Cockapoo origins, characteristics, and health considerations for prospective and current owners.
historyText: The Cockapoo emerged in the 1960s as one of the first 'designer dogs,' created by crossing Cocker Spaniels with Poodles to combine the best traits of both breeds.
firstDesignerBreed: One of the first designer dog breeds
crossbreedPurpose: Bred for intelligence and low-shedding coat
sizeWeightText: Toy: 6-12 lbs, Miniature: 13-18 lbs, Standard: 19-24 lbs
coatColorsText: Black, brown, red, cream, white, parti-color, phantom, merle
coatTextureText: Ranges from straight to curly, typically wavy and soft
averageLifespanText: 12-15 years with proper care, nutrition, and regular veterinary checkups.
dentalCare: Regular dental care and teeth cleaning
detailedCareDescription: Everything you need to know about grooming, nutrition, exercise, and training your Cockapoo for optimal health and happiness.
mattingPrevention: Pay special attention to legs and ears
eyeCleaning: Clean eyes daily with damp cloth
earCareDaily: Check ears for debris and odor
professionalCuts: Puppy cut, teddy bear cut, or full coat
puppyNutritionText: Growing puppies need high-quality food with proper protein and fat ratios for healthy development.
highQualityPuppyFood: Premium puppy formula with real meat
growthFormula: DHA for brain and eye development
adultNutritionText: Adult Cockapoos need balanced nutrition to maintain energy levels and healthy weight.
portionControl: 1/2 to 1 cup daily, divided into 2 meals
exerciseNeedsText: Cockapoos are moderately active dogs that need daily physical activity to stay healthy and happy.
mentalStimulationText: Mental exercise is just as important as physical activity for this intelligent breed.
healthVeterinaryDescription: Essential health information, common diseases, vaccination schedules, and preventive care guidelines for your Cockapoo.
earInfectionsText: Cockapoos are prone to ear infections due to their floppy ears that trap moisture and debris.
earInfectionsPrevention: Prevention: Weekly cleaning, keep ears dry after baths
skinProblemsText: Allergies and skin sensitivities can cause itching, redness, and hot spots.
skinProblemsPrevention: Prevention: Quality diet, regular grooming, allergy testing
dentalHealthText: Small dogs are prone to dental issues including tartar buildup and tooth loss.
dentalHealthPrevention: Prevention: Daily brushing, dental chews, regular cleanings
rabiesBooster: Rabies booster (1-3 years depending on local laws)
optionalVaccines: Optional: Bordetella, Lyme disease, Canine influenza
puppyDeworming: Puppies: Deworming every 2-3 weeks until 6 months
adultDeworming: Adults: Annual fecal exam and deworming as needed
optimalTimingText: Generally recommended between 6-12 months, consult with your veterinarian for the best timing.
behaviorImprovement: May reduce marking and roaming behaviors
breedingConsiderationsText: Only breed healthy, genetically tested dogs with good temperaments.
puppyCheckupsText: Every 3-4 weeks until 16 weeks old for vaccinations and health monitoring.
adultCheckupsText: Annual wellness exams including blood work and dental evaluation.
seniorCheckupsText: Bi-annual checkups for dogs over 7 years old to catch age-related issues early.
appetiteChanges: Changes in appetite or water consumption
lifestyleInteractionDescription: How Cockapoos interact with family members, other pets, and adapt to different living situations and travel.
withChildrenText: Cockapoos are excellent with children of all ages, known for their gentle and patient nature.
withCatsText: Generally good with cats, especially when socialized early. Proper introduction is key.
catIntroductionTips: Tip: Introduce gradually and supervise initial interactions
withOtherDogsText: Usually friendly with other dogs due to their social nature and good breeding.
dogSocializationTips: Tip: Early socialization helps ensure good dog-to-dog relationships
apartmentLivingText: Well-suited for apartment living due to their moderate size and adaptable nature.
moderateSize: Compact size suitable for smaller spaces
houseWithYardText: Thrives in homes with yards where they can play and explore safely.
climateConsiderationsText: Adaptable to various climates but may need protection in extreme weather.
carSafety: Use proper car restraints or carriers
stressReduction: Consider calming aids for anxious dogs
accommodationsText: Research pet policies and fees in advance, bring vaccination records.
separationAnxietyText: Cockapoos can develop separation anxiety due to their strong bond with owners.
positiveAssociation: Create positive associations with alone time

landing.heroDescription: Connect databases and use SQL, AI, and visualization to explore, analyze, and reuse.
landing.heroSubtitle: Query databases, understand tables, and talk to data - <brand>All in One</brand> modern workspace.
landing.heroExperienceTitle: No download needed, experience Dory in 30 seconds
landing.heroExperienceDescription: Use a public dataset to experience SQL + AI

aiChat.title: AI copilot
aiChat.description: Generate SQL and explain results from the current connection, schema, and query context.

sqlConsole.title: SQL workbench
sqlConsole.description: Keep the editor, schema browser, query results, and execution history in one workflow.

teamFlow.title: Save & reuse
teamFlow.description: Save useful queries and result context so one-off exploration becomes a reusable workflow.

aiNative.heading: <ask>Ask.</ask> <act>Act.</act> <stay>Stay grounded.</stay>
aiNative.description: No context switching. No hallucinated fields. Just accurate SQL and real results.

ask.label: Ask
ask.title: Ask anything. No made-up columns.
ask.description: Schema-aware autocomplete and SQL generation keep the AI honest — every suggestion is based on real tables, real fields, and real joins.

actions.label: Actions
actions.title: Do more than chat — take action.
actions.description: Run transformations, save and share queries, and trigger common analytics tasks right from the workflow — no copy-paste, no tab hopping.

context.label: Context
context.title: Context that keeps you in control.
context.description: Dory tracks what you’re working on — tables, relationships, and recent queries — so results stay consistent across Ask, Action, and Context.

tableOverview.title: Auto table intelligence that frames every column and distribution.
tableOverview.description: Automatically generates table-level overviews, semantic annotations, and usage cues so AI understands business data before it writes SQL.

summary.title: Auto summary & semantics
summary.description: Dory reads counts, freshness, and relationships to describe what a table holds and why it matters.

fields.title: Field meanings & signal
fields.description: Each column gets a short semantic note plus hints about ranges, cardinality, and helpful joins.

context.title: Distribution cues & SQL context
context.description: Data distributions, skew, and business notes supply the grounded context AI needs for trustworthy SQL.

chatbot.title: Chat with Your Database
chatbot.description: Dory’s Chatbot is not a generic AI assistant. It’s a database-native copilot that understands your schema, writes real SQL, and helps you explore data through conversation.

callout.label: Live chat
callout.title: Grounded answers, every time
callout.description: Queries are written with real fields, real joins, and real confidence.

coreWorkspace.title: Built for real SQL work — <brand>not just chat</brand>.
coreWorkspace.description: A fast editor, a trustworthy schema view, and saved work you can reuse — the foundation of daily data work.

highlights.item3: High-performance result grids with sorting, filtering, and fast navigation.
highlights.item4: Designed for large data volumes and complex analytics.

editor.title: SQL Editor that feels native
editor.description: Fast, keyboard-first editing with schema-aware autocomplete — so you can write correct SQL without breaking flow.

schema.title: Schema Explorer you can trust
schema.description: Browse tables, columns, and types in context — the same grounding the AI uses to avoid hallucinated fields.

saved.title: Saved Queries & Results
saved.description: Turn one-off exploration into a workflow. Save, revisit, and share the queries that matter.

databaseSupport.title: Connect the data stack you already use.
databaseSupport.description:
Supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, MariaDB, Neon, and ClickHouse.
A modern workspace for querying, exploring, and analyzing your data.

moreFeatures.title: SQL workspace designed for everyday data work

secure.title: Local-first security
secure.description: Queries, personal settings, and workflows stay on your computer so privacy and control never leave your hands.

connectivity.title: SSH Tunneling
connectivity.description: SSH tunnels wrap every connection in strong encryption, so your credentials, queries, and data never leave your network in plain text.

save.title: Save Queries & Workflows
save.description: Save queries, context, and results so you can rerun and build on work again and again.

modern.title: Modern experience
modern.description: A clean, restrained interface that keeps you immersed in the rhythm of your work.

themes.title: Bright / Dark & Multi Themes
themes.description: Light, dark, and multiple accent themes adapt to your environment while keeping the visuals readable and comfortable.

open-source.title: Open Source
open-source.description: Apache 2.0 licensed, extensible, and ready for self-hosting.

cta.title: Start now
cta.subtitle: The AI-native SQL workspace for modern databases.
cta.description: Download Dory now, or deploy it locally with Docker and connect it to your real database.
cta.tip: After startup, add your database connection in the UI.

answer.paragraph1: Yes. Dory provides a free version that you can use for personal projects and local workflows.
answer.paragraph2: As the project evolves, we will explore paid capabilities and team features, but the core SQL workspace will remain free.

aiCopilot.question: How does the AI Copilot work?

answer.paragraph1: Dory’s AI Copilot understands your database schema, tables, and query context to help generate SQL, explain queries, and explore data faster.
answer.paragraph2: Instead of generic prompts, Dory provides structured context from your workspace so AI responses are more accurate and practical.

answer.paragraph1: The desktop version comes with OpenAI models built in for a better out-of-the-box experience. The open source version supports multiple model providers through configurable environment variables, including OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, and more.
answer.paragraph2: You can also route requests through your own AI Gateway or enterprise infrastructure.

answer.paragraph1: Dory currently supports ClickHouse, PostgreSQL, Neon, MySQL, MariaDB, and SQLite, with a deeply integrated experience for ClickHouse.
answer.paragraph2: DuckDB is planned, and we will continue expanding support to provide a consistent AI-native workspace across modern databases.

answer.paragraph1: Dory is licensed under Apache 2.0, is developer-friendly, and can be self-hosted.
answer.paragraph2: We welcome community contributions, whether in code, documentation, or new feature ideas.

answer.paragraph1: Dory follows a local-first philosophy. Your connections and workspace metadata can stay on your machine when using the desktop version.
answer.paragraph2: AI features only send the minimum required context based on your configuration. At the moment, Dory uses OpenAI models.

deployment.heading: Run Dory where <brand>your data</brand> lives.
deployment.description: Connect to real databases and deploy in your environment — local, Docker, or fully self-hosted.

databases.title: Databases
databases.shield: No forced uploads. Dory runs in your environment and talks to your database over the connections you control.

deployment.title: Deployment
deployment.description: Choose local-first speed or self-hosted control.

local.title: Local app
local.description: Run on your machine for the fastest, simplest setup.

docker.title: Docker
docker.description: Self-host in minutes with a single container.

selfhost.title: Self-hosted
selfhost.description: Deploy alongside your database in your own environment.

cloud.title: Your cloud
cloud.description: Run where your data lives — VPC, VPN, or internal network.

deployment.extra: Works with private networks (VPC/VPN) and self-managed databases. You keep the keys, the network, and the data path.
deployment.footer: Want a database integration next? <open>Open an issue on GitHub</open>.
